GOBD Information

Blaise Diagne International Airport (French: Aéroport International Blaise Diagne) is an international airport near the town of Diass in Thiès Region, Senegal, 43 kilometres (27 mi) east of downtown Dakar. It serves as the main airport for Dakar, replacing Léopold Sédar Senghor International Airport (GOOY), which had become too small. It is named after Blaise Diagne, the first black African elected to France's parliament in 1914. More information available at Wikipedia.

ICAO GOBD
IATA DSS

Transition Altitude 3500 ft
Transition Level FL40
